queries.term: ("machine learning"[Title/Abstract] OR "deep learning"[Title/Abstract]) AND (genomics OR proteomics OR "drug discovery" OR "computational biology")

papers.title: Trends in the Application of Multiomics Based on Machine Learning in the Development of Probiotics.
papers.journal: Journal of agricultural and food chemistry
summary_bullets: Here are the three bullet points summarizing the biomedical abstract:
ML-assisted applications have emerged in probiotic research with rapid development of computational methods and high-throughput multiomics technologies.

ML facilitates integration of heterogeneous omics data for systematic and quantitative probiotic development, enabling functional prediction, metabolic activity prediction, and probiotic effectiveness optimization.

papers.title: A framework for identifying the polyploid complex in Rorippa (Brassicaceae): combining trait evolution, herbarium records, and machine learning.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
Seed arrangement, petal number, and genome size exhibited clear interspecific differentiation.
Phylogenomic analyses based on chloroplast genomes further resolved species circumscription consistent with these traits.

papers.title: High MAP3K6 Expression in Spinal Cord Injury Tissues Enhances Neuronal Apoptosis: Insights from Multi-Omics Data Integrating WGCNA, Machine Learning, and Experimental Validation.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
The study analyzed gene expression data from spinal cord injury (SCI) patients and identified 4,621 differentially expressed genes.
Functional enrichment analyses revealed that the differentially expressed genes were involved in protein degradation pathways, immune-related processes, and ubiquitin-mediated proteolysis.

papers.title: Genome characterization of cluster EK1 bacteriophages JessellCookie and MiamiPanther.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
Bacteriophages JessellCookie and MiamiPanther were isolated from environmental samples.
The phages have a podoviral morphology.

papers.title: Unraveling the genome of Epidendrum fulgens: demographic history and gene family dynamics in a resilient Neotropical orchid.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
The first chromosome-scale genome assembly is presented from the orchid genus Epidendrum, specifically E. fulgens.
The genome assembly captured 97.3% of BUSCO genes and consisted mostly of transposable elements, with approximately 30,830 protein-coding genes identified.

papers.title: Possible cross‑talk between sarcopenia and obstructive sleep apnea revealed by WGCNA analysis and machine learning.
papers.journal: The aging male : the official journal of the International Society for the Study of the Aging Male
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
The study identified 20 shared common genes associated with the comorbidity of sarcopenia and obstructive sleep apnea.
LGR6 and ARRDC2 were found to be candidate hub genes for diagnosing comorbid OSA and sarcopenia.

papers.title: Post translational modifications as biomarkers of soil microbe responses to nano-pesticides.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
Nano-pesticides offer improved target specificity and reduced chemical load in agriculture.
Post-translational modifications (PTMs) serve as functional biomarkers for nano-pesticide-induced microbial stress.

papers.title: Validation of important genes and bioinformatics investigation of the relationship between ferroptosis and CVA16 infection.
summary_bullets: No abstract available.

papers.title: Uncertainty-aware hybrid deep generative framework for robust and explainable drug discovery.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
A novel hybrid computational framework is introduced to generate drug molecules with desired pharmacological properties.

The framework integrates Graph Convolutional Networks, Variational Autoencoders, and Uncertainty Aware Adaptive Multi-Objective Optimization-based Reinforcement Learning for reliable and interpretable results.

papers.title: Prosta-omics: machine learning-driven TPSA prediction and molecular modeling of ASPM-inhibitors for prostate cancer treatment.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
Single-cell RNA sequencing (scRNA-seq) was used to analyze prostate cancer tissues and identify cell-type-enriched biomarkers.
ASPM emerged as a statistically significant biomarker associated with proliferative tumor phenotypes, and drug-like molecules were curated and docked against a 3D model of ASPM.

papers.title: Integrating ensemble machine-learning and fibril docking to discover potent, novel triazole-naphthalene tau-aggregation inhibitors.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
A QSAR model was trained to predict IC50 values for human-tau aggregation inhibition.
The model predicted that 16 previously unreported derivatives of 1,2,4-triazole-naphthalene (TNDs) have potential as tau-aggregation inhibitors.

papers.title: Cell-free chromatin state tracing reveals disease origin and therapy responses.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
The cf-EpiTracing platform profiles histone modifications in cell-free DNA to accurately deconvolve cell types of origin.
The platform enables unbiased identification of primary diseased tissues and other organ involvement, as well as detection of early-stage diseases or lesions.

papers.title: AI can write genomes - how long until it creates synthetic life?
summary_bullets: No abstract available.

papers.title: Cell-free DNA fragmentomes for noninvasive detection of liver cirrhosis and other diseases.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
A machine learning classifier detected early liver disease, advanced fibrosis, and cirrhosis with high sensitivity in separate discovery and validation cohorts.
Genome-wide fragmentome and methylome analyses revealed liver-derived and immune-mediated changes in cfDNA in individuals affected with liver disease.

papers.title: Deep learning linking mechanistic models to single-cell transcriptomics data reveals transcriptional bursting in response to DNA damage.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
The study proposes an inference framework called DeepTX that connects mechanistic models and single-cell RNA sequencing (scRNA-seq) data to reveal genome-wide transcriptional burst kinetics.

Applying DeepTX to several scRNA-seq datasets, the researchers found that fluctuations in transcriptional bursting induced by different drugs were associated with distinct fate decisions: differentiation, apoptosis, or survival.

papers.title: Systemic Proteome Profiling to Differentiate Primary Glomerular Diseases.
papers.journal: Journal of the American Society of Nephrology : JASN
summary_bullets: Here are the three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
Plasma proteome profiles were distinct among disease subtypes.

The machine learning model performed robustly in both discovery and validation cohorts, achieving an AUROC > 0.8 for differentiating minimal change disease, membranous nephropathy, and IgA nephropathy.

papers.title: Decoding d- and l-Amino Acids: Data-Driven Recognition of Enantiomers and Post-Translational Modifications via Quantum Tunneling.
papers.journal: The journal of physical chemistry letters
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
The authors combine machine learning with quantum tunneling to identify d- and l-amino acid enantiomers and post-translational modifications.
This approach screens for chirality-specific molecular signatures within complex proteomes, addressing limitations in existing methods.

papers.title: In-Pocket 3D Graphs Enhance Ligand-Target Compatibility in Generative Small-Molecule Creation: A Dopamine D2 Receptor Model System.
papers.journal: The journal of physical chemistry. B
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
The graph-based generative model encodes explicit 3D protein-ligand contacts in a relational graph architecture.
Molecules generated by this 3D procedure are more compatible with the dopamine D2 receptor-binding pocket than those produced by a comparable 2D method.

papers.title: Deep generative molecular design and its value in modern drug discovery.
summary_bullets: Here are the three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
Neural network-based frameworks enable algorithms to propose molecules that satisfy complex pharmacological constraints.

Generative AI's greatest promise lies in generating better hypotheses, structures that are synthetically accessible, biologically plausible, optimized across potency, selectivity, and pharmacokinetics.

papers.title: miR-1290 serves as a potential biomarker for osteoporotic fracture and regulates osteoblast function by targeting KDM5A.
papers.journal: Journal of orthopaedic surgery and research
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
Serum miR-1290 levels decreased from healthy controls to osteoporotic fracture (OPF) patients.
miR-1290 promoted osteoblast proliferation and differentiation, and its expression was upregulated during osteogenic differentiation.

papers.title: Identification of metabolism and inflammation-related biomarkers and potential drugs for schizophrenia based on comprehensive bioinformatics and machine learning.
summary_bullets: No abstract available.

papers.title: Genome-wide identification of castor bean OSCA genes and analysis of expression patterns under drought and salt stress.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
A total of 10 OSCA genes (RcOSCAs) were identified in castor bean using bioinformatics approaches.
Phylogenetic analysis classified the RcOSCA proteins into four distinct subfamilies and clarified their evolutionary relationships with homologs from other plant species.

papers.title: Identification of the disulfidptosis-related gene G6PD as a potential biomarker and therapeutic target in pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma by integrative bioinformatics analysis and experimental validation.
summary_bullets: No abstract available.

papers.title: Identification and characterization of Phi1 and Phi3 bacteriophages targeting Xylella fastidiosa subsp. pauca, the causal agent of olive quick decline in Italy.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
Xylella phage Phi1 is a novel podovirus with a genome size of 44,345 bp.
Phi3 is likely the same species as Xylella phage Salvo and exhibits morphological features typical of siphoviruses.

papers.title: Benzo[a]pyrene promotes gastric cancer progression via activation of the Correa cascade through modulation of the STAT3-TP53-MMP9 molecular axis.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
The genes TNF, IL6, IFNG, IL1B, and STAT3 were identified as hub genes driving the Correa cascade during gastric cancer development.
The genes STAT3, TP53, and MMP9 were found to be core targets of Benzo[a]pyrene (BaP) and exhibited synergistic expression patterns in tumors.

papers.title: Plasma proteomic profiles of lung volume-based phenotypes in Tobacco-Exposed Individuals Without Spirometric COPD.
papers.journal: Annals of the American Thoracic Society
summary_bullets: The study found that individuals with elevated total lung capacity (TLC) have distinct biological features reflected in their circulating proteome.
Among pre-COPD subgroups, those with an elevated functional residual capacity-to-TLC ratio (FRC/TLC) showed greater progression to GOLD ≥ 2 and PRISm.
The study confirmed the reproducibility and prognostic validity of lung volume-based phenotypes in predicting COPD development over a 5-year follow-up period.

papers.title: Proteomic profile of CSF obtained at the time of diagnosis determines amyotrophic lateral sclerosis progression and survival: CXCL7 levels in disease prognosis and survival.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
The study identified complementary cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) biomarkers and pathway-specific signatures through a non-targeted multiomic approach.
CXCL7 emerged as a promising complementary biomarker, which robustly distinguished ALS patients from controls and stratified patient subgroups by survival.

papers.title: [Mechanism of miR-199a-5p prevents migration and invasion of cholangiocarcinoma by targeting hypoxia-inducible factor 1α].
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the biomedical abstract:
miR-199a-5p is expressed in intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma (ICC) tumor tissues.
Hypoxia-inducible factor 1α (HIF-1α) is a target of miR-199a-5p and its expression is regulated by miR-199a-5p.

papers.title: Intergenerational neurotoxicity mediated by maternal plasma metabolite profile remodeling following maternal atrazine exposure.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
Maternal exposure to atrazine during pregnancy and lactation can damage dopaminergic neurons in offspring.
The study revealed that maternal atrazine exposure remodels the maternal plasma metabolic profile, leading to intergenerational dopaminergic neurotoxicity in offspring.

papers.title: AlleleMiner: A long-read pipeline for gene-wise de novo allele phasing and variant detection in diploid citrus cultivars.
papers.journal: DNA research : an international journal for rapid publication of reports on genes and genomes
summary_bullets: AlleleMiner is a Python-based pipeline that phases diploid gene sequences directly from PacBio HiFi reads.
The pipeline achieved an average phasing output of 91.5% of single-copy genes across 18 citrus cultivars.
A ∼30× HiFi depth is preferable for the stable recovery of heterozygous alleles, reducing potential allele dropout.

papers.title: Linking protein aggregation and structural stability to predict pathogenic MYH7 variants via machine learning.
summary_bullets: Disease-causing variants in Myosin Storage Myopathy generally destabilize the MYH7 α-helical coiled-coil domain.
Pathogenic variants cluster in locally unwound regions of the coiled-coil dimer, promoting dimer destabilization and aggregation.
The RDSM-MYH7 machine learning-based predictor accurately assesses the pathogenicity of missense mutations in the MYH7 rod domain.

papers.title: Assessment of In-Frame Indel Variants in an Unsolved Cohort of Inherited Retinal Diseases Using Machine Learning.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
MetaRNN-indel showed the best overall results among four machine learning (ML) tools evaluated.
MetaRNN-indel identified two likely pathogenic causal variants in two unrelated IRD patients by confirming clinical phenotypes.

papers.title: Genomics of Sable (Martes zibellina) × Pine Marten (Martes martes) Hybridization.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
The study resequenced 30 individuals from sables' and pine martens' ranges to investigate hybridization and detect a broad spectrum of hybrid types with mosaic recombinant chromosomes.
Hybrid fertility was confirmed, and mitochondrial DNA introgression from sables into most pine martens was detected, indicating displacement of native pine marten sequences.

papers.title: Germline-specific nascent polypeptide-associated complex (NAC) subunits are predicted by bioinformatics tools to undergo phase separation.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
The germline NAC (gNAC) proteins in Drosophila melanogaster have distinctive molecular features that promote phase separation.
These features include an atypical amino acid composition, low-complexity repeats, and extended disordered regions that may undergo structuring upon oligomerization.

papers.title: L-Se-methylselenocysteine alleviates iron deposit-induced anxiety- and depression-like behaviors in chronic renal failure rats receiving iron therapy via Nrf2/GPX4 activation.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
ESRD patients showed higher Beck Depression Inventory scores than healthy controls.
QSM revealed increased magnetic susceptibility in the ventromedial prefrontal cortex and hippocampus, positively correlated with depression severity.

papers.title: Integrative chromosome-scale genome analysis of cupuassu provides insights into witches' broom disease resistance and expands genomic resources for Theobroma.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
The genome of Cupuassu's WBD-resistant genotype C174 is presented, featuring distinctive duplications, TE insertions, and coordinated defense-gene expression.
Compared to the susceptible genotype C1074, C174 shows higher heterozygosity, differential TE expression, and spatial proximity to immune loci, suggesting regulatory importance.

papers.title: Leveraging Artificial Intelligence to Advance Bioinformatics in Africa: Opportunities, Challenges, and Ethical Considerations in Combating Antimicrobial Resistance.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
AI-powered models can predict AMR patterns from whole-genome sequencing data, reducing turnaround times from days to hours.
AI algorithms such as ML and DL can automate analysis of large-scale sequence datasets, predicting resistance patterns and modeling infection transmission dynamics.

papers.title: Identification of Genetic Variants Among Breast Cancer Patients and At-Risk Individuals: A Cohort Study in Sri Lanka.
papers.journal: Breast cancer : basic and clinical research
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
Variants were detected in several high- and moderate-penetrance genes, including BRCA1, BRCA2, PALB2, BRIP1, and MRE11A.
Among these variants, BRCA2 mutations and a PALB2 frameshift deletion were classified as pathogenic germline variants.

papers.title: Bioinformatic Analysis and Experimental Verification Reveal the Protective Role of Baicalein Against LPS-Induced Endothelial Cell Dysfunction.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the biomedical abstract:
Baicalein (BAI) exhibits protective effects against lipopolysaccharide (LPS)-induced endothelial cell injury.

BAI modulates the inflammatory response and immune microenvironment by regulating genes including vascular cell adhesion molecule 1 (VCAM1), phosphoinositide-specific phospholipase C X-domain containing 1 (PLCXD1), and MIR3142 host gene (MIR3142HG).

papers.title: Elucidating the mechanisms of aristolochic acid-induced upper tract urothelial carcinoma: A multi-omics approach combining bioinformatics and computational modeling.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
The study identified 97 shared potential targets of Aristolochic acids (AAs) and Upper Tract Urothelial Carcinoma (UTUC).
Topological analysis highlighted five core genes: CASP3, EGFR, PARP1, PTGS2, and HSP90AA1, which showed high binding affinities with AAs.

papers.title: Unraveling the Phylogenetic Signal of Gene Expression from Single-cell RNA-seq Data.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
ScAllele, Monopogen, and Monovar strategies consistently called single-nucleotide variants from scRNA-seq data.
These approaches provided phylogenetically informative genotype calls that discriminated between tumor and normal cells within samples and distinct subclonal lineages over time.

papers.title: A Real-Time, Programmable, and Multiplex Ligation Chain Reaction-MNAzyme Platform for Single-Nucleotide Variant Detection.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
The platform integrates ligase chain reaction (LCR) with multicomponent nucleic acid enzymes (MNAzymes) for single-nucleotide variant (SNV) analysis.
The system enables real-time quantitative monitoring of SNVs while supporting programmable and multiplex detection through a universal MNAzyme-based reporter.

papers.title: DoBSeqWF: a framework for sensitive detection of individual genetic variation in pooled sequencing data.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
DoBSeq is a cost-effective method that uses two-dimensional overlapping pool sequencing to enable individual-level rare variant detection.
The resulting high-depth, complex data require a specialized workflow, which was developed into a Nextflow-based pipeline called DoBSeqWF.

papers.title: ANOMALY: a Snakemake pipeline for identifying NuMTs from long-read sequencing data.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
The ANOMALY workflow detects nuclear mitochondrial DNA segments (NuMTs) from long-read sequencing data.
The pipeline demonstrated high accuracy on 50 simulated datasets, with a precision of 1.000, recall of 0.989, and F1-score of 0.994.

papers.title: Dual Confinement-Enhanced Multiple Single Nucleotide Variant Detection at the Single-Particle Level.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
A novel strategy was introduced for analyzing multiple single nucleotide variants (SNVs) at the single particle level.
The method uses DNA tetrahedra to enhance sample background tolerance, X-shaped probes on DNA tetrahedra, and a hybridization chain reaction cascade confined to gold nanoparticles.

papers.title: NGSTroubleFinder: a tool for detection and quantification of contamination and kinship across human NGS data.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
NGSTroubleFinder detects cross-sample contamination in human whole-genome and transcriptome sequencing data.
The tool can also identify sample swaps and mismatches between reported and inferred genetic and transcriptomic sexes.

papers.title: MobiCT: a UMI-based circulating tumor DNA analysis pipeline.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
The bioinformatics pipeline MobiCT detects ultra-low-frequency variants in cell-free DNA samples using unique molecular identifier (UMI).
MobiCT consists of three stages: UMI deduplication, alignment to a reference genome, and variant calling.
